channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: JCI Manila's ColorFest Carnival to Benefit Mental Health Services
item.description: JCI Manila, known as Asia's leading leadership development organization, is partnering with Hyve Sports to launch the JCI Manila ColorFest Carnival Series 2026. This exciting event, happening on May 3...

item.title: Narok Senator Ledama Olekina Expresses Interest in ODM Secretary General Role
item.description: Narok Senator Ledama Olekina has officially announced his interest in taking up the position of Secretary General (SG) in the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM). Speaking on April 5, 2026, Ledama emphas...

item.title: Plunder charges against Escudero and Romualdez expected to be filed in May
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman has announced that it is preparing plunder charges against Senator Chiz Escudero and Leyte Representative Martin Romualdez in connection with a flood control scandal. These...

item.title: Dodgers Return to Toronto for Three-Game Series Against Blue Jays
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers return to Toronto to face the Blue Jays in a three-game regular season series, marking a rematch of last year's World Series. While Toronto has worked to strengthen its roster ...

item.title: Nigerian troops free 31 hostages after Easter attack on church in Kaduna
item.description: The Nigerian army has successfully rescued 31 hostages who were abducted during an attack on a church in Ariko village, Kaduna state, on Easter Sunday. The attack occurred during an Easter service at ...

item.title: Kejriwal to Represent Himself in Delhi HC Recusal Plea; Next Hearing on April 13
item.description: In the ongoing excise policy scam case, Delhi's former Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal has expressed his intent to argue his recusal application personally in front of the Delhi High Court. This statem...

item.title: Long-serving Waitrose employee dismissed for intervening during Easter egg theft
item.description: Walker Smith, a 54-year-old Waitrose employee with 17 years of service, was dismissed from his role at the Clapham Junction branch after confronting a shoplifter attempting to steal Easter eggs. Accor...

item.title: Kenya Police Push Joint Efforts to Tackle Farm Theft and Secure Agriculture
item.description: The National Police Service (NPS) has urged stronger partnership between security agencies and the private sector to address a growing problem of farm theft in Kenya. According to a statement from NPS...

item.title: Supreme Courts of Pakistan and Turkiye enter judicial collaboration to promote legal exchange and capacity building
item.description: The Supreme Court of Pakistan has signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Turkiye’s Constitutional Court aimed at deepening judicial cooperation between the two countries. The signing ceremony...

item.title: Federal government to cover Rs6bn cost to keep Pakistan Railways fares stable till June
item.description: The federal government of Pakistan has decided to bear an additional financial burden of roughly Rs6 billion to ensure that Pakistan Railways (PR) does not raise passenger fares or freight charges ove...

item.title: Investigation Underway Over Claims of Rent Collection from RDP House Beneficiaries
item.description: The Gauteng Department of Human Settlements has launched an investigation into claims made by a Nigerian man in a viral video. In the footage, the man claims to be collecting rent from RDP house benef...

item.title: Tanzania Urged to Verify News to Protect Peace and Unity
item.description: In Tanzania, authorities and experts are emphasising the importance of distinguishing between real news and fake news to maintain national peace and unity. Fake news, especially on social media, has b...

item.title: Indian Oil boosts Auto LPG supply in Karnataka and advises temporary petrol use due to West Asia tensions
item.description: The Indian Oil Corporation (IOC) Limited has intensified the supply of Auto Liquefied Petroleum Gas (Auto LPG) across Karnataka in response to a sudden shortfall caused by continuing geopolitical tens...

item.title: Iran and US Consider Ceasefire Amid Escalating Conflict and Assassination of Iranian Intelligence Chief
item.description: The Guardian reports an intensifying crisis between Iran, the United States, and Israel, as tensions surge in the ongoing war. Iran’s intelligence chief, Major General Majid Khademi of the Revolutiona...

item.title: Sienna Toohey wins 100-metre breaststroke title at 2026 Australian championships
item.description: Sienna Toohey, a 17-year-old swimming prodigy from Albury, has made waves in Australian swimming after clinching the women's 100-metre breaststroke title at the 2026 Australian Open-Age Swimming Champ...

item.title: Ceasefire Proposal Circulated Amid Escalating U.S.-Iran Conflict with Dozens Killed
item.description: Israel and the United States carried out coordinated air strikes in Iran on Monday, resulting in the deaths of more than 25 people, including Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi, head of intelligence for Iran’s R...

item.title: Religious Leaders in Zanzibar Call for Peace and Unity During Easter
item.description: Religious leaders in Zanzibar have urged Tanzanians to uphold peace, unity, and social cohesion as essential for national stability and growth. The Anglican Bishop of Zanzibar, Rt Revd Michael Hafidh,...

item.title: New Youth Centres Aimed at Tackling Anti-Social Behaviour in Hotspot Areas
item.description: The UK government is launching a series of new youth centres, the first of which will open in locations such as Bristol, Nottingham, and Leeds. These 'Young Futures Hubs' aim to offer young people age...

item.title: Cody Walker offers support to Nathan Merritt during cancer battle
item.description: NRL star Cody Walker has sent heartfelt messages of support to Nathan Merritt as the former Rabbitohs player battles cancer. Walker, who played alongside Merritt during his career, took to social medi...

item.title: Thomas du Toit proves his dominance in Bath's Champions Cup comeback win over Saracens
item.description: Thomas du Toit, known as 'The Tank,' delivered a game-changing performance for Bath in their dramatic 31-22 win over Saracens in the Champions Cup. Coming off the bench at halftime, Du Toit helped tur...
